exemplar.title: The Rise Of Non-Binary Pronouns In English: Their Integration In Formal And Informal Discourse And The Reflection Of Societal Perceptions

detailedExemplarData.title: The Rise Of Non-Binary Pronouns In English: Their Integration In Formal And Informal Discourse And The Reflection Of Societal Perceptions

subject.title: English B
subject.description:
**IB English B** is a **Group 2** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **language acquisition through five prescribed themes: identities, experiences, human ingenuity, social organization, and sharing the planet**, with a strong focus on **developing receptive, productive, and interactive communication skills in English as a second language**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Paper 1** | Productive skills - writing | 1 hour | 1 hour 15 minutes | 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Receptive skills - listening and reading comprehension | 1 hour 45 minutes | 2 hours | 50% |
| **Individual Oral** | Internal assessment - oral communication | 15 min prep + 10 min exam | 15 min prep + 10 min exam | 25% |

strengths: Clear and engaging communication of the topic with precise purpose and focus
Explicitly stated, focused research question revisited throughout the essay
Conclusion succinctly reinforces research question and main findings
weaknesses: Methodology outline lacks detail on selection and evaluation criteria for sources
Title is somewhat verbose, affecting conciseness
Transitions in some sections do not consistently tie back to the research question
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates accurate and effective topic communication and a clearly stated, focused research question with strong coherence (bands 5â6). However, the methodology is only mostly complete without explicit criteria for source selection (band 3â4). Balancing these, a score of 5 is warranted to reflect high performance in focus and question clarity, tempered by partial methodological transparency.

strengths: Coherent and clear subject knowledge with accurate use of key sociolinguistic terms
Generally relevant and appropriate application of a wide range of sources
Effective integration of historical and policy-related examples to frame the argument
weaknesses: Depth of treatment is occasionally surface-level, with complex theories mentioned but not fully explored
Some references to linguistic mechanisms are vague
Occasional over-reliance on popular media sources without critical embedding
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay shows mostly clear and coherent knowledge and appropriate application of source material, meeting the solid 3â4 band descriptors. Occasional lapses in depth and precision prevent placement in the highest band, justifying a score of 4.

strengths: Research sources are relevant and clearly connected to the research question
Insightful ideological analysis of resistance and effective contextual comparisons
Logical development of arguments linking policy adoption to language change
weaknesses: Counter-arguments (e.g., free-speech concerns) are underdeveloped and lack depth
Analysis often remains descriptive rather than probing complexities
Claims about generational divides lack empirical support
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Critical thinking is solidly in the 4â6 band: research relevance is clear and consistent, arguments are coherent, but evaluation lacks depth and empirical substantiation. A mid-band score of 5 accurately reflects these strengths and limitations.

strengths: Clearly appropriate and complete overall structure following EE conventions
Complete table of contents and logical section headings aiding navigation
Presence of key layout elements such as title page and references
weaknesses: Page numbering is not consistently visible, impeding navigation
Typographical artifacts and abrupt transitions reduce readability
Dense paragraphs would benefit from subheadings for clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation meets the criteria for a clearly appropriate and complete structure but is marred by formatting lapses and readability issues. This corresponds to a solid 3 in the 1â2 (layout) and 3â4 (structure) band, averaged to 3.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is explicitly stated on the title page and re-quoted in the introduction.  Although slightly long, it is focused on two measurable variables (influence on usage; reflection of perceptions) and these threads are revisited in every main section.  Because the connection between the question and the planned discussion is clear and maintained, the work aligns with the 5-6 band.

Analytical passages (e.g., comparison of historical and contemporary usage; linking policy to language change) show logical development, yet they often remain descriptive and do not probe competing linguistic theories or quantitative data.  The reasoning is adequate and mostly relevant but lacks the nuance expected for the upper bands, meriting a lower-mid score within 4-6.

Cited materials (Baron 2020; Curzan 2003; legal statutes; media articles) are relevant and usually integrated into the argument, demonstrating more than minimal appropriation.  Some sources are popular journalism and are sometimes summarised rather than critically embedded, so the application is âmostlyâ but not uniformly strongâplacing it in the 3-4 range at the upper end.

Key layout elements (word count, title, table of contents, section headings, references) are present.  Page numbering is implied but not visible in the provided extract, and some typographical artifacts ({0} etc.) suggest minor formatting issues.  These small imperfections fit the descriptor of âpresent and correctly appliedâ with slight lapses, so 3 of 4 is appropriate.

The essay shows clear awareness of linguistic history, prescriptive vs. descriptive grammar, and sociolinguistic principles.  Explanations are coherent, but depth is occasionally surface-level (e.g., complex theories of language change are mentioned but not explored).  The generally accurate but not advanced treatment corresponds to a solid 3-4 performance.

The essay follows the required EE structureâtitle page, abstract substitute, table of contents, numbered sections, conclusion and reference listâdemonstrating a clearly appropriate and complete organisation.  Transitions are logical, so the strand fulfils the top presentation band (3-4) at full marks.

The bibliography and in-text citations indicate purposeful research across academic works, legal documents and media sources that are clearly related to the RQ.  Some over-reliance on secondary reportage limits excellence, but the relevance and range surpass âpartialâ and align with âclear relevanceâ, justifying a mid-band (4-6) placement.

The student states a clearly identifiable topicââthe rise of non-binary pronouns in Englishâ â and immediately situates it within the broader themes of formal / informal discourse and social perception.  The introduction, section headings and repeated linking back to inclusivity show an unambiguous purpose.  Minor wordiness (âpeople-centered inclusivityâ) does not impede clarity, so communication is accurate and generally effective, matching the 5-6 descriptor.

The student sustains a coherent line of argument that the adoption of non-binary pronouns is both mirror and driver of social change.  Nonetheless, evaluation of counter-arguments (e.g., free-speech concerns) is brief and conclusions occasionally restate rather than interrogate evidence.  This results in a âgoodâ but not âexcellentâ discussion, appropriate for the 4-6 band at 4 marks.

A broad outline of method is offered (historical survey, media analysis, policy review) and a reference list shows deliberate source selection.  However, there is no explicit explanation of how data were gathered or evaluated (e.g., no criteria for choosing case studies, no primary research).  This partial but not fully articulated methodology fits the âmostly completeâ description of the 3-4 band.

Key terms such as âsingular they,â âneo-pronouns,â âprescriptive grammar,â and âinstitutional recognitionâ are used accurately and consistently.  Occasional lapses in precision (ânon-binary pronouns are neutral pronouns that are usedâ¦â) do not undermine overall correctness, so the strand meets the âmostly accurateâ descriptor (3-4 band).

annotations.comment: The title clearly communicates the investigationâs focus on non-binary pronouns within discourse and societal perceptions, establishing a precise and engaging purpose.
The inclusion of a precise word count and a clearly stated research question demonstrates strong adherence to formatting requirements.
The title is somewhat verbose; consider tightening phrasing to improve readability and maintain concise focus.
Discussion of media and institutional adoption offers clear relevance to your research question.
Sections listing examples read descriptively without deeper evaluation of their broader implications or contradictions.
The transition into the resistance discussion lacks an explicit tie back to the central research question.
Discussion of institutional adoption lacks engagement with possible critiques or negative outcomes.
The conclusion succinctly revisits the research question and main findings, reinforcing focus and coherence.
The conclusion is repetitive and could be trimmed to avoid restating points already covered in the main body.
Condense the conclusion by focusing on key evidence and avoiding redundant language.
The table of contents is complete and logically organized, facilitating easy navigation through the essayâs sections.
Page numbering is not visible in the footer, which may impede clear reference and navigation.
The opening paragraph offers a compelling context and demonstrates proficient integration of source material to frame the study.
The methodology outline lacks detail on how sources were selected and evaluated, weakening the transparency of your research process.
Consider specifying criteria for choosing case studies or policy documents and detailing how you evaluated their relevance to your question.
The historical background section effectively situates linguistic evolution within socio-cultural change, showing clear subject knowledge.
The reference to âerosion of noun gender inflectionsâ is vague; clarify the linguistic mechanisms or influential texts involved to strengthen accuracy.
The narrative of pronoun development is coherent and shows a solid grasp of linguistic history.
The section ends abruptly with âThisâ¦â, suggesting missing text or lack of smooth transition to the next segment.
The first contemporary paragraph effectively cites academic and style guides, illustrating solid application of source material.
The definition of non-binary pronouns is circular; refine it to explain distinct features or usage contexts beyond neutrality.
Reaffirm the research questionâs elements in section headings to maintain focus and cohesion throughout chapters.
The discussion of companies and platforms is well chosen and highlights the role of policy in language change.
Citation formatting is inconsistent (e.g., <math>2020</math> embedded inline); standardize all references to meet style guidelines.
Long, dense paragraphs on page 11 impede readability; consider breaking into smaller thematic units.
Incorporate subheadings to delineate key arguments and improve the flow of complex discussions.
The ideological analysis of resistance is insightful and demonstrates effective critical engagement.
Claims about generational divides lack supporting data or statistics; this weakens the argumentâs evidential basis.
Incorporate empirical survey results or quantitative studies to substantiate the generational divide claim.
The comparison to past lexical shifts (e.g., firemanâfirefighter) effectively contextualizes resistance.
Examples are largely anecdotal; integrating empirical studies on institutional resistance would deepen analysis.
The section on legal and educational advances provides clear evidence of policy impact on language.
The report lacks any reflective section on research planning or decision-making, missing the required engagement strand.
Recap of institutional initiatives is comprehensive and effectively demonstrates subject knowledge.
The methodology section still lacks reflection on limitations and decision-making; add a reflective commentary.
Incorporate a reflective section detailing planning decisions, setbacks, and adaptations to demonstrate analytical engagement.
References include a balanced mix of academic and media sources, showing comprehensive research.
Citation entries vary in format and lack consistent retrieval dates; standardize reference styling across all entries.
The bibliography on page 18 thoroughly covers relevant literature and enhances credibility.
No page number is visible in the footer of this page, reducing ease of reader orientation.
